title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard led the Boston Celtics with 36 points in a 109-102 victory over the Atlanta Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points, 12 rebounds, and five assists in the game, despite not fully finding his shot.
The Celtics benefited from a controversial foul call on Nickeil Alexander-Walker, impacting the game's final minutes.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics this season after a ruptured Achilles' tendon, motivated by the team's championship potential.
Tatum was cleared by medical staff and returned for the final regular season stretch, contributing double-doubles for the Celtics.
The Celtics are currently in second place, aiming to secure a home playoff series with Tatum providing strong rebounding and versatility.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was fatally shot after an altercation between two armed individuals in a Lowell apartment complex.
The shooting occurred around 4 p.m. at a residence on Pawtucket Boulevard, according to the Middlesex DA's Office.
The incident is under investigation by the Middlesex DA, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police Department.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston for the 2027 season after being purchased by the Rockets' Fertitta family.
The WNBA team will be renamed the Houston Comets, a tribute to the city's former team disbanded in 2008.
Steve Pagliuca's previous $325 million attempt to move the Sun to Boston was reportedly blocked by the WNBA.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' starring Ana de Armas is streaming on HBO Max.
'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice', a time-travel action-comedy with Vince Vaughn, is available on Hulu.
'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 featuring Charlie Cox and Krysten Ritter is streaming on Disney+.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown is out with left Achilles tendinitis for the Celtics' game against the Atlanta Hawks.
Coach Joe Mazzulla states Brown's status is 'day-to-day' after his recent treatment session.
Derrick White and Neemias Queta are available, while Nikola Vucevic remains out with a finger fracture.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington have enacted bans on gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
A Boston.com poll shows 73% of over 250 respondents oppose the bans, citing practicality and limited environmental impact.
Supporters of the bans highlight issues with noise, fumes, and health impacts in densely populated areas.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The new COVID variant BA.3.2, nicknamed 'cicada,' has appeared in Massachusetts wastewater samples.
BA.3.2, first found in South Africa in 2024, evades antibodies from 2025-2026 vaccines.
WHO reports BA.3.2 doesn't increase severe disease or hospitalization rates compared to other variants.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled a lawsuit can proceed against two DCF workers involved in the seizure of children in Waltham.
Sarah Perkins and Josh Sabey allege their children were removed without consent despite no evidence of abuse.
Judge Patti Saris dismissed several parties from the lawsuit but retained liability for DCF's Butterfield and Gemski.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Willie's on Beacon Hill, led by Jamie Bissonnette, features global dishes and pizzas, now open in Boston.
Ladyfish in Portland, Maine, offers a summer pop-up focusing on Maine seafood with Chinese and Italian influences from May.
La Tavernetta by Douglass Williams will open in East Boston, emphasizing coastal tavern fare with oysters and spritzes.
